examples: I'm definitely coming to the party.
This is definitely the best pizza I've ever had.
You should definitely try the cheesecake.
context: How can you choose a book that is appropriate for your level? For beginners, I would recommend graphic novels because not only is the text usually a bit more short and simple, but they also provide images. You can understand the context and what's happening in the book a little bit more easily. For intermediate learners, I recommend choosing chapter books or shorter young adult novels. These are definitely a step up from the graphic novels because they usually don't have images, but the text is written for teenagers or for first readers. So the language is usually a little bit more simple and approachable. Young adult is actually a whole genre in the US. So if you search young adult novels, you will get so many options. But in a bit, I will share some of my favorite recommendations with you. And for advanced learners, we really need to choose something that is interesting to you. You could also find something that you relate to. For example, maybe something related to your job. One app that I really recommend using is called Goodreads because I can research different books that I'm interested in and see what other people are saying about it. I would say if there is at least four stars, then you know it's a great book because book critics are very harsh. So if it's got four stars, it's bound to be good. Now that you've learned how to choose the correct book, I'm going to teach you how to read to help improve your English. When you're reading and you come to a word that you don't know, don't look it up in the dictionary right away. I think this is a mistake that many people make. They overuse their dictionary while reading. So if you come to a word that you don't know, first, I would try to guess what it is based on the context in the book alone. Once you've highlighted it, forget about it for a minute.

examples: Focus on pronunciation, not just grammar.
context: Next, I recommend whenever you finish reading for the day to write at least a one or two sentence summary about what you've read. This will help you retain all of the information that you learned from reading. And it will also help you preview and think about what will come next. Finally, if you want to take it a step further and practice your English listening and speaking along with your English reading, I would recommend doing the following things. First, you can practice reading the book aloud. It helps train your mouth to say these things out loud. Number two, try downloading an audio book. This will improve your listening as you follow along in the book and pay attention to the pronunciation. This is also a good chance for you to improve your speaking as well because you can chattel. So listen to the audio book and say it out loud as you follow along. So this is an excellent method because you are reading, listening, and speaking all at the same time.
